text: A model with high {{c1::bias}} underfits the data, while high {{c2::variance}} overfits and fails to generalize.
explanation: Bias-variance tradeoff is a core concept for choosing models, regularization, and validation strategies.
usage: Fundamental for supervised learning, model selection, and debugging poor performance.
spanish: Un modelo con alto sesgo subajusta los datos, mientras que alta varianza sobreajusta y falla en generalizar.

text: Cross-validation estimates generalization by repeatedly splitting data into train and validation folds.
explanation: It reduces the risk of reporting optimistic performance from a single lucky split.
usage: Used in model comparison, hyperparameter tuning, and feature engineering workflows.
spanish: La validación cruzada estima la generalización al dividir los datos en múltiples folds de entrenamiento y validación.

text: A {{c1::confounder}} can create a misleading association between a feature and the target if it is not controlled.
explanation: Confounding is central for causal reasoning and for avoiding false conclusions in experimentation.
usage: Important in observational studies, marketing analysis, and policy evaluation.
spanish: Un factor de confusión puede crear una asociación engañosa entre una característica y la meta si no se controla.

text: Regularization adds a penalty to limit model complexity and reduce overfitting.
explanation: L1 and L2 regularization are standard tools for controlling variance in linear and neural models.
usage: Essential in logistic regression, SVMs, and deep learning optimization.
spanish: La regularización añade una penalización para limitar la complejidad del modelo y reducir el sobreajuste.

text: In imbalanced datasets, metrics like {{c1::precision}}, {{c2::recall}}, and {{c3::F1}} are often more informative than raw accuracy.
explanation: Class imbalance can make accuracy nearly useless when one class dominates the dataset.
usage: Common in fraud detection, rare disease prediction, and anomaly detection.
spanish: En conjuntos con clases desbalanceadas, métricas como precisión, recall y F1 suelen ser más informativas que la exactitud pura.

text: Feature engineering transforms raw variables into representations that expose relevant structure to the model.
explanation: Good features often matter more than the algorithm itself in practical forecasting and tabular problems.
usage: Central in credit scoring, churn prediction, and recommendation systems.
spanish: La ingeniería de características transforma variables crudas en representaciones que exponen estructura relevante al modelo.

text: A calibrated model outputs probabilities that reflect real-world confidence rather than just ranking examples.
explanation: Calibration is essential for risk estimation, medical diagnosis, and decision support.
usage: Used when probabilities matter more than hard labels.
spanish: Un modelo calibrado devuelve probabilidades que reflejan la confianza real en lugar de solo ordenar ejemplos.

text: Distribution shift occurs when the data seen in production no longer matches the training distribution.
explanation: This is one of the most common causes of degraded real-world ML performance.
usage: Critical for deployment monitoring, forecasting, and fraud systems.
spanish: El cambio de distribución ocurre cuando los datos vistos en producción ya no coinciden con la distribución de entrenamiento.
